title: Default and Direct Exchanges, Work Queues, and Fair Dispatch
description: A task service publishes through the default exchange without naming the coupling, assumes one queue broadcasts, uses auto-ack workers, and calls round-robin dispatch fair despite unequal task cost. Learners cumulatively produce direct-routing work distribution service through theory, guided work, debugging, transfer, retrieval, and assessment.
objectives: Explain the default exchange queue-name binding and choose it only when direct queue-name coupling is intentional.
Design direct exchange bindings that keep producer intent stable while consumer queues evolve.
Use one queue with multiple consumers for load distribution and state that each delivery goes to one active consumer at a time.
